english: nyál-bu (nyal bu) bastard, whoreson Ma. - nyál-sa (nyal sa) sleeping-place.

1. num. fig.: 38. - 2. num. inst. of gnyis (gnyis) in compounds, nyi-brgyá (nyi brgya), -stóṅ (-stong), -khrí (-khri) etc., nyi-khrí (nyi khri) also title of a book, the Prajñā Pāramitā, containing 28 000 Śloka. - 3. for nyi-ma (nyi ma).

(Bal. *nyó-ma* (*nyo ma*), 1. the sun, ཤར་ śar (shar) becomes visible, rises; śar (shar) id., also: has risen, shines; nub (nub), rgas (rgas), W. also *skyod, bud* (*skyod, bud*), sets, is setting; nyi-ma nub tse bar (nyi ma nub tshe bar) (for tseï bár-du (tshe'i bar du)) until sun-set Sch.; nyi-mai gnyen (nyi ma'i gnyen). akin to the sun, the Sākya race Cs.; *da nyi-ma riṅ-mo* (*da nyi ma ring mo*) W. now the sun stands already high in the heavens; *nyi-ma-gaṅ-śár* (*nyi ma gang shar*) sun-flower, Helianthus. — 2. day, = nyín-mo (nyin mo), opp. to night, frq.; *nyi-ma-tse* (*nyi ma tshe*) W. the whole day, all day long; *nyi-ma-péd* (*nyi ma phyed*) W. noon, mid-day; nyi-ma gcig (nyi ma gcig) one day, once Dzl.: nyi-ma-re-rér (nyi ma re rer) daily. Comp. nyi-dkyíl (nyi dkyil) disk of the sun Sch. — nyi - gúṅ (nyi gung), nyi - mai guṅ (nyi ma'i gung) noon, mid - day; meridian (?) Cs. — nyi - dgá (nyi dga') seems to be the n. of a medicinal herb Med. — nyi-rgás (nyi rgas) sun-set. — nyi-ldóg (nyi ldog) the solstice, dgún-nyi-ldog (dgun nyi ldog) winter solstice, dbyár - nyi - ldog (dbyar nyi ldog) summer solstice Wdk. — nyi-núb (nyi nub) = nyi-rgás (nyi rgas). — nyi-tsé (nyi tshe) 1. Sch.: the time or duration of one day. 2. Lex.: = pradeśa direction, place, country (?); nyi-tsé spyód-pa (nyi tshe spyod pa) Lex.: a kind of ascetic; nyi-tsé-ba (nyi tshe ba) Sch.: ephe-meral; single, simple; Thgy.: n. of a class of infernal beings. — nyi - tsód (nyi tshod) sun - dial, nyi - tsód - kyi kór-lo (nyi tshod kyi 'khor lo) the circle of a sun-dial Cs. — nyi-ḥdzin (nyi 'dzin) eclipse of the sun (cf. sgra-gcàn (sgra gcan)). — nyi-zér (nyi zer) sun-beam, nyi-zér rtá-la ཞོན་ nas (rta la zhon nas) riding on a sun-beam Mil. and elsewh.; nyi - zér - gyi rdul (nyi zer gyi rdul) a mote floating in a sun-beam. — nyi-zlá (nyi zla) sun and moon; also the figures of sun and moon connected, crowning the top of the mčod-rtén (mchod rten); nyi-zlá bsdad mi oṅ (nyi zla bsdad mi 'ong) sun and moon will not stand still Mil. — nyi-ḥóg (nyi 'og) below the sun; the earth Was. (49); nyi-ḥóg-gi rgyal-khams (nyi 'og gi rgyal khams) Glr. id.; it seems, however, to denote a certain country, acc. to Mahāvyutpatti the same as Aparāntaka, Williams: the western country; cf. Schf. on Tar. 92. — nyi-ḥód (nyi 'od) sun-shine. — nyi-yól (nyi yol) any screen or shelter from the sun's rays: awning, curtain, parasol, pent-house Sch.; *nyi-rib* (*nyi rib*) (prop. sgrib (sgrib)) W. id., umbrella. — nyi-śár (nyi shar) sun-rise Cs. — nyi-lhag (nyi lhag) Sch. a cold day (?) — Cf. nyín-mo (nyin mo).

(inst. of nyis-ču (nyis bcu)), often in conjunction with thám - pa (tham pa), twenty, nyi-śu-rtsa-gcíg (nyi shu rtsa gcig) B., C., *nyi-śu-nyer-gcíg* (*nyi shu nyer gcig*) W., nyer-gcíg (nyer gcig), twentyone.
W. loose, slack, lax, not tight or tense.
Ssk. मण्ड Cs.: 'heart, spirit, essence', cf. snyíṅ-po (snying po).
Sch.: sure, trustworthy, Lex.: nyiṅ-tor (nying tor) = ṅes-par (nges par).

a category not familiar to us; gen. mentioned together with yán-lag (yan lag); it might be translated by: members of a second order, parts of the yán-lag (yan lag); the exact meaning must however remain undetermined, as the Tibetans themselves are not able to give a clear definition of it. In C.: inner parts of the body, opp. to outer. In books, phrases like the following are to be found: yán-lag (yan lag) daṅ (dang) nyiṅ-lag (nying lag) tams-cád (thams cad) daṅ (dang) ldán-pa (ldan pa); yán-lag (yan lag) daṅ (dang) nyiṅ-lag (nying lag) ná-ba (na ba); yán-lag (yan lag) daṅ (dang) nyiṅ-lag (nying lag) gcód-pa (gcod pa); evidently the nyiṅ-lag (nying lag) are smaller, but more numerous than the yán-lag (yan lag). In Pth. also nyiṅ-sprúl (nying sprul) is found besides yaṅ-sprúl (yang sprul), emanation of the third order; v. sprúl-pa (sprul pa).

1. self, same, opp. to other persons, ma nyid (ma nyid) the mother herself Dzl.; mi de ni rgyál-po nyid yín-no (mi de ni rgyal po nyid yin no) this man are you yourself, o king! Dzl.; the very, just he, just it etc., las byéd-paï gnas nyíd-la (las byed pa'i gnas nyid la) just where I am working Dzl.; deï druṅ-nyíd-na (de'i drung nyid na) (or du (du)) close by, to, or before, hard by, Thgy.; dus de - nyíd - du (dus de nyid du) at the very moment, frq.; mčód-bya nyid (mchod bya nyid) that which is venerable par excellence Tar. 15, 13; yón-
